title: Sr Manager Technical Accounting | Remote
summary: You'll work directly with a portfolio of high-growth Pre-IPO and publicly traded clients, supporting them on complex accounting issues, SEC filings, and IPO readiness initiatives. This role is ideal for someone who wants broad client exposure, meaningful impact, and a clear path for advancement.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
items: Prepare and/or review SEC filings, including 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1, S-4, MD&A, and footnotes
Lead or support technical accounting research and draft memos on complex topics (ASC 606, ASC 842, ASC 805, equity, derivatives, etc.)
Assist with IPO readiness, audit preparation, internal controls, and reporting processes
Work directly with client CFOs, Controllers, auditors, and legal teams
Manage multiple engagements in a fast-paced advisory environment
Depending on level, lead projects and mentor junior team members

title: Tax Manager | Public Accounting Firm in Great Neck NY
summary: We're a growing CPA firm serving small businesses, entrepreneurs, real estate investors, and medical & dental practices — and we need a Manager who can do more than push paper. You'll be the person our clients actually look forward to hearing from. The one who explains a complex tax situation without making their eyes glaze over. The one who catches things others miss and manages a workflow that hums.
Think of this role as part tax expert, part client whisperer, part team anchor. If that sounds like you — keep reading.

sections.heading: What you'll actually do
items: Review complex individual, business, and entity tax returns — and catch what the software missed
Analyze financial statements and translate them into plain English your clients will actually understand
Serve as the day-to-day point of contact for a portfolio of business clients — building real relationships, not just completing transactions
Manage workflow and deadlines across a team — keeping everyone on track without losing your cool during busy season
Proactively educate clients on tax-saving opportunities, planning strategies, and what's changing in their world
Mentor and support junior staff — the kind of manager who helps people grow, not just delegates
Handle real estate, small business, and medical/dental practice engagements with confidence

title: VP Operations/COO | Lakewood NJ
summary: Our client supports therapy providers with state-specific billing operations. The company is growing quickly, and the CEO is seeking a strong operational leader to take over day-to-day management and streamline all billing and back-office processes.

The ideal candidate brings deep RCM experience within ABA therapy billing, strong managerial skills, and an ability to scale processes through automation and global workforce integration (India and Colombia).

sections.heading: Key Responsibilities
items: Own and manage all day-to-day operations of the RCM business supporting ABA providers.
Directly oversee, mentor, and develop a team of five billing specialists.
Manage state-specific billing for ABA services, including four primary billing codes.
Implement process improvements to increase speed, accuracy, and cash flow.
Identify and drive automation opportunities across the revenue cycle.
Build and manage teams offshore (India, Colombia) to support scaling needs.
Create reporting, KPIs, and operational dashboards to support executive decision-making.
Partner closely with the CEO to remove operational bottlenecks and enable the CEO's focus on new business development.
Ensure compliance with state Medicaid rules, payer requirements, and ABA billing guidelines.

title: CFO | Lakewood NJ
summary: Our ABA client is seeking a strategic, hands-on Chief Financial Officer to build and lead the company's finance function from the ground up.
The CFO will partner directly with executive leadership to drive financial strategy, build infrastructure, improve visibility, and support growth across multiple facilities.

sections.heading: Key Responsibilities
items: Build the finance function, including accounting processes, reporting frameworks, budgeting, and internal controls.
Oversee monthly close, financial statements, cash flow management, and operating metrics.
Develop scalable systems and tools that support multi-location healthcare operations.
Lead financial planning & analysis, forecasting, and scenario modeling.
Implement revenue cycle processes, reimbursement oversight, payor management, and healthcare billing workflows.
Establish KPIs and dashboards to support operational decision-making.
Work closely with the CEO on capital planning, funding needs, and strategic initiatives.
Manage banking, audit, tax, and external financial relationships.
Eventually build and lead a small finance team as the company grows.

title: Director of Revenue Cycle | Remote
summary: We're looking for a Director of Revenue Cycle to lead our end-to-end revenue cycle function as we build and scale our outpatient specialty infusion model.

items: Lead the full revenue cycle process from referral intake through payment resolution.
Oversee benefits verification, prior authorizations, financial clearance, billing, coding, claims, collections, denials, and appeals.
Build scalable workflows, SOPs, dashboards, quality checks, and escalation processes.
Manage and develop team members supporting intake, authorization, billing, coding, collections, and AR follow-up.
Monitor key metrics including days in AR, denial rate, clean claim rate, cash collections, authorization turnaround time, and net collection rate.
Partner with clinical and operations teams to ensure documentation supports accurate billing and timely reimbursement.
Identify payer, process, documentation, and system issues that impact reimbursement and lead improvement efforts.
Provide oversight to payer enrollment, credentialing, contracting, fee schedule review, and new-site launch readiness.
Ensure compliance with HIPAA, CMS, payer requirements, billing rules, and internal policies.

title: Head of Growth - ABA - Long Island
summary: The Head of Growth will be responsible for identifying, launching, and scaling new ABA clinics across the United States. This role focuses on growth strategy, deal-making, partnerships, payer expansion, and M&A opportunities, ensuring the company successfully enters new states and accelerates clinic rollouts. You will own market selection, growth playbooks, competitive intelligence, and strategic relationships that fuel national expansion.

sections.heading: Market & State Expansion
items: Build and execute the company's multi-state expansion roadmap, identifying high-value new markets based on demographics, demand, payer attractiveness, and competitive gaps.
Conduct deep market analyses to determine clinic viability, projected caseloads, and payer dynamics.
Lead the regulatory and payer-readiness process for entering new states.

sections.heading: Clinic Growth & Launch Strategy
items: Develop replicable playbooks for launching new facilities including market validation, staffing models, payer readiness, and ramp-up forecasts.
Partner with operations to ensure clinics scale efficiently from opening to maturity.
Drive continuous optimization of growth KPIs including enrollment velocity and clinic ROI.

sections.heading: Business Development & Partnerships
items: Build and maintain relationships with referral networks, school districts, pediatricians, health systems, and community organizations.
Secure strategic partnerships that accelerate patient acquisition and market penetration.
Expand payer relationships to improve contracting opportunities in targeted states.

sections.heading: M&A & Strategic Investments
items: Source, evaluate, and model potential acquisitions including independent ABA providers and small clinic groups.
Lead financial analysis, due diligence, valuation, and integration recommendations for M&A.
Develop frameworks to assess joint ventures, tuck-in acquisitions, and de novo vs. buy decisions.

sections.heading: Growth Infrastructure & Analytics
items: Build dashboards and analytics to track performance of new markets, clinics, and acquisitions.
Develop forecasting tools for clinic ramp-up, state saturation mapping, and growth ROI modeling.
Translate data insights into executive recommendations to guide strategic decisions.

title: Senior Accountant - Construction Company - Westchester NY (On-Site)
summary: Our client is a $40M+ construction engineering and services supply company operating across the U.S. and Canada as a strategic, free-standing segment of a larger private family fund. With strong financial backing and ambitious plans for organic and acquisition-driven growth, the business is entering a major scaling phase. They are now seeking a Senior Accountant to support the Head of Finance and help strengthen the company's expanding accounting function.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
items: Lead month-end and year-end close activities, including journal entries, reconciliations, and analysis.
Maintain the general ledger and ensure accuracy across accounts.
Prepare and review balance sheet reconciliations, resolving discrepancies proactively.
Manage fixed asset accounting, including additions, disposals, depreciation.
Reconcile bank accounts, monitor cash activity, and manage foreign currency (forex) transactions, including revaluations and reporting of exchange gains/losses.
Perform intercompany reconciliations, eliminations, and settlement processes across multiple entities.
Support accounts payable processing and ensure compliance with internal controls.
Coordinate project and monthly billing, including use of Textura with the Project Management team.
Prepare budgets and forecasts, providing analysis and insight for management review.
Serve as a point of contact for external auditors: plan, prepare lead schedules, and provide support.
Ensure compliance with U.S. GAAP, internal controls, and company policies.
Identify and implement process improvements to increase efficiency and strengthen reporting.

title: Clinical Director - ABA Company in Savannah GA
summary: Our client has built from the ground up to be client inspired, parent guided, and clinically informed. They seeks a new Clinical Director to lead their Savannah GA facility.

sections.heading: How we value you:
items: Inclusive culture
Competitive compensation
Career progression
Benefits
Collaborative environment
Perks
Continuing education opportunities

sections.heading: Clinical Director, BCBA (Board Certified Behavior Analyst) Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
items: You'll leverage internal and external resources and support of the performance of your role
You'll provide clinical oversight and guidance to the clinic's BCBAs
Manage clinic escalations
Meet a small case load requirement
Be present in the clinic 5 days a week working remotely on designated 'Flex Days'
You'll work closely with Co-Founders on leadership responsibilities and meeting KPIs
You'll hold bi-weekly 1:1 meetings (~15-30 minutes) with each BCBA in the clinic to offer support, coaching, oversight and feedback.
You'll host a bi-weekly team meeting to align on goals, address needs, and build connection.
Oversee the clinic's day to day
You'll send out a monthly clinic update to your team, sharing key wins, progress towards the clinic's goals, shoutouts, and reminders.
You will provide clinical oversight, including through parallel supervision, Learning Tree and Client Review meetings, and by upholding clinical standards.
Provide other job functions as may reasonably arise in the performance of ABA duties, as directed by company management.

title: Tax Senior or Manager - West Palm Beach or Fort Lauderdale - Hybrid
location: Palm Beach or Fort Lauderdale - Hybrid
summary: Our public accounting client is looking for Senior Tax Accountants and Managers to work on their family office, real estate, and C-Corp clients.

items: Client Relationship Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for a portfolio of clients. Cultivate and strengthen relationships by providing exceptional service, anticipating needs, and delivering proactive tax planning and consulting advice.
Technical Tax Expertise: Oversee and review complex tax returns for various entity types, including:
Family Offices & High-Net-Worth Individuals (HNWI): Comprehensive planning and compliance for trusts, estates, gifts, and sophisticated individual returns (Forms 1040, 1041, 709).

Real Estate Entities: Partnership and S-corporation returns (Forms 1065, 1120-S) with expert knowledge of partnership tax allocations, basis calculations, passive activity limitations, and like-kind exchanges (IRC Section 1031).
Corporations: C-corporation compliance and planning (Form 1120), including consolidated returns and multi-state tax issues.

Tax Planning & Consulting: Identify and implement complex tax strategies to minimize client tax liabilities. Advise clients on structuring transactions, compliance changes, and other strategic initiatives.

Team Leadership & Development: Mentor, train, and develop senior and staff accountants. Manage workflow, ensure adherence to deadlines and quality standards, and foster a collaborative team environment.

title: BCBA/LBA - St. Louis Missouri
summary: A growing ABA organization is seeking an experienced and compassionate BCBA/LBA to join the clinical team. This role involves providing high-quality, evidence-based ABA services to children across home and school settings while collaborating closely with families, RBTs, and multidisciplinary teams.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
items: Conduct functional behavior assessments (FBAs) and develop individualized behavior intervention plans (BIPs)
Oversee and supervise RBTs and/or behavior technicians, including ongoing training and performance feedback
Deliver direct ABA services as needed and ensure treatment fidelity
Monitor client progress through data collection, analysis, and program modification
Collaborate with families, educators, and related service providers to support client goals
Participate in IEP meetings, school consultations, and team meetings as required
Maintain accurate, timely clinical documentation in compliance with BACB, state, and payer requirements
Adhere to ethical standards and best practices as outlined by the BACB

title: Director of Intake - ABA Therapy - New Jersey (hybrid)
summary: Our client is seeking an experienced Intake Director to lead and optimize the full intake lifecycle for a growing ABA therapy organization. This role oversees a team of 12 and ensures families move through the intake and authorization process smoothly, efficiently, and with exceptional service.

sections.heading: Key Responsibilities:
items: Lead, mentor, and manage a 12-person intake team; drive performance, accountability, and professional development.
Oversee all inbound inquiries, eligibility checks, benefit verification, authorizations, and onboarding processes for new families.
Ensure intake SLAs, quality metrics, and communication standards are consistently met.
Partner cross-functionally with clinical, scheduling, billing, and operations teams to ensure seamless start-of-care.
Own intake workflow optimization, documentation, and reporting within Zoho CRM; identify system enhancements to improve accuracy and throughput.
Monitor pipeline trends, capacity, and bottlenecks; provide weekly reporting and recommendations to leadership.
Maintain compliance with HIPAA, payer requirements, and ABA industry standards.

title: Senior Accountant/Supervisor - Nursing Home Back-Office - Long Island, NY
summary: Our Nursing Home back-office client is seeking a detail-oriented Senior/Accounting Supervisor with 1–6 years of experience in healthcare environment and contribute to accurate financial reporting and operational excellence.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
items: Prepare and deliver monthly financial reports and related deliverables.
Oversee a team off offshore staff in production of financials.
Perform account reconciliations and ensure accuracy across all ledgers.
Assist with month-end and year-end close processes.
Support budgeting and forecasting activities as needed

title: AP Manager - Woodmere NY/Skilled Nursing
summary: Our client is building a best-in-class back-office infrastructure for Skilled Nursing Facilities, delivering disciplined financial operations and strong internal controls across a growing portfolio. They're looking for their next Manager of AP.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
items: Build and oversee a centralized Accounts Payable function supporting multiple SNF clients
Manage high-volume invoice processing, coding, approvals, and timely payment execution
Establish AP policies, procedures, and internal controls to ensure accuracy and compliance
Oversee vendor setup, W-9 collection, contract compliance, and vendor relationships
Partner with facility administrators and corporate leadership to resolve invoice discrepancies
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations, audit requirements, and company controls
Implement and optimize AP systems, workflows, and automation tools
Lead, mentor, and scale the AP team as the platform grows
Produce AP aging, cash forecasting, and management reporting

title: Director of Acquisitions - Investment Management - Long Island, NY/Hybrid
summary: Lead the sourcing, underwriting, and execution of investment opportunities, driving portfolio growth in industrial acquisitions across core, value-add, and opportunistic strategies. This hybrid role partners closely with senior leadership to deliver disciplined, data-driven investment outcomes.

items: Source, evaluate, and execute acquisition opportunities with a primary focus on driving portfolio growth in industrial assets.
Lead full-cycle deal execution including underwriting, financial modeling, due diligence, and investment committee presentations.
Develop and maintain relationships with brokers, owners, lenders, and operating partners to generate proprietary and off-market deal flow.
Analyze market trends, supply/demand dynamics, and asset-level fundamentals to inform acquisition strategy and pricing.
Negotiate letters of intent, purchase and sale agreements, and coordinate with legal, finance, and third-party advisors through closing.
Collaborate with asset management to support business plans, capital deployment, and value-creation initiatives post-acquisition.
Monitor portfolio performance and provide ongoing reporting and insights to senior leadership and investors.
Contribute to strategic planning, investment thesis development, and expansion of the firm's industrial acquisition platform.

title: BCBA
summary: Atlanta-based BCBA role with 90%+ remote flexibility and just two on-site school visits per month.

items: 90%+ remote work with a predictable, low on-site requirement
Only 2 on-site days per month at a partnered school in Atlanta
Focus on program oversight, data review, and collaboration (not heavy daily in-person hours)
Ideal for BCBAs seeking flexibility without sacrificing meaningful school-based impact
Supportive client environment with clear expectations and structured scheduling
